title: Derivative as Slope of Tangent Line

flashcards.definition: f'(a) = lim_{h→0} [f(a+h)−f(a)]/h; the instantaneous rate of change at a.
A line touching the curve at one point with slope equal to the derivative.
y − f(a) = f'(a)(x − a); point-slope form using the derivative.
As h → 0, the secant line through two nearby points becomes the tangent line.
A function is differentiable at a if f'(a) exists (the limit exists).
